name: Cajun Sausage Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix granulated garlic, dried chives, parsley flakes, and restaurant black pepper to make MYSEASONINGS mix. Add salt to the mix.
2. Cut the pork loin into small roasts, about 6 inches long. Make a hole through the center of each roast along the central axis.
3. Insert the smoked sausage into the holes. If the cross-section of the roast is large enough, cut two parallel holes.
4. Open the holes to fit the smoked sausage and pour about 1 tablespoon of MYSEASONINGS into the long holes. Use something to push through the roasts to distribute the seasoning evenly inside and outside.
5. Slide the smoked sausage through the holes until about 1 inch protrudes from each end. Dust the roasts with seasonings and salt. Allow them to marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes.
6. Place the roasts on a rack in a covered roaster. Pour the chopped onion and bell pepper into the bottom of the roaster and place it, uncovered, in a preheated oven at 425°F for approximately 30 minutes or until lightly seared on the outside.
7. Cover the roaster and continue cooking with a meat thermometer until the internal temperature reaches 160-165°F. Remove the roasts, trim any excess sausage from the ends, and chop fine.
8. Dissolve cornstarch in cold water and stir into the soy sauce mixture. Cook over medium heat to thicken the sauce, adjusting color with Kitchen Bouquet and seasonings as needed.
